title: Mapania parvibractea
taxonomy: Mapania parvibractea is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is a part of the genus Mapania, which is a group of sedges that are mainly found in tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The family Cyperaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that are commonly known as sedges or rushes. Mapania parvibractea, like other members of the Cyperaceae family, is characterized by its grass-like appearance and tiny flowers. The classification of Mapania parvibractea is further detailed in the plant's binomial nomenclature, with its scientific name indicating its unique classification.

The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism.
